title: Wilt Thou, O Man, Live Happily

metadata.composer: 'Mensch, Willt du Leben Seligich' attr. Martin Luther. Found in Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein, 1524 Johann Hermann Schein, 1627
metadata.lyricist: Martin Luther, 1524.  Translated by Richard Massie, 1854, alt
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Martin Luther, 1524.  Translated by Richard Massie, 1854, alt.
Music: 'Mensch, Willt du Leben Seligich' attr. Martin Luther. Found in Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein, 1524.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.

1.raw_text: Wilt thou, O man, live happily, And dwell with God eternally, The ten commandments keep, for thus Our God Himself biddeth us. Kyr' eleison!

2.raw_text: I am the Lord and God! take heed No other god doth thee mislead; Thy heart shall trust alone in Me, My kingdom then thou shalt be.

3.raw_text: Honor My Name in word and deed, And call on Me in time of need: Hallow the Sabbath, that I may Work in thy heart on that day.

4.raw_text: Obedient always, next to Me, To father and to mother be; Kill no man: even anger dread; Keep sacred thy marriage bed.

5.raw_text: Steal not, nor do thy neighbor wrong By bearing witness with false tongue; Thy neighbor's wife desire thou not, Nor grudge him aught he hath got.
